title: The Power of Habit: How Small Changes Can Transform Your Life
title_zh: 习惯的力量：小改变如何改变你的生活
intro_zh: 本文约 1000 词，探讨习惯对生活的影响，以及如何通过小改变建立积极习惯，适合 CET-4 水平。

paragraphs.en: Have you ever wondered why some people seem to achieve their goals effortlessly while others struggle? The answer often lies in their habits. According to research, about 40% of our daily actions are driven by habits, not conscious decisions. This means that by changing our habits, we can experience significant improvements in our fitness, productivity, and overall happiness. Understanding how habits work is the first step to taking control of your life.
paragraphs.zh: 你有没有想过，为什么有些人似乎毫不费力地实现目标，而另一些人却苦苦挣扎？答案往往在于他们的习惯。根据研究，我们每天约40%的行为由习惯驱动，而非有意识的决定。这意味着通过改变习惯，我们可以在健康、生产力和整体幸福感方面体验显著改善。了解习惯如何运作是掌控生活的第一步。

paragraphs.en: A habit consists of three parts: a cue, a routine, and a reward. For example, many people feel stress after work (cue), then they go for a run (routine), and finally they feel relaxed (reward). This cycle becomes automatic over time. The key to changing a habit is to keep the same cue and reward but replace the routine with a healthy one. For instance, instead of eating junk food when stressed, you could take a short walk.
paragraphs.zh: 一个习惯由三部分组成：提示、惯常行为和奖赏。例如，很多人下班后感到压力（提示），然后去跑步（惯常行为），最后感到放松（奖赏）。这个循环随着时间的推移会变成自动的。改变习惯的关键是保持相同的提示和奖赏，但用健康的惯常行为代替原来的。例如，当你感到压力时，与其吃垃圾食品，不如散散步。

paragraphs.en: One powerful way to build new habits is to start with small changes. James Clear, author of Atomic Habits, suggests the "2-minute rule": start by doing a new habit for just two minutes. Want to read more? Read one page. Want to exercise? Put on your sneakers. These small actions may seem insignificant, but they build momentum. Once you start, it becomes easier to continue. The secret is to make the habit so easy that you can't say no.
paragraphs.zh: 培养新习惯的一个有效方法是从小改变开始。《原子习惯》的作者詹姆斯·克利尔建议“两分钟规则”：开始时只做两分钟的新习惯。想多读书？读一页。想锻炼？穿上运动鞋。这些小的行动可能看起来微不足道，但它们能积累动力。一旦开始，继续下去就更容易了。秘诀是让习惯变得简单到无法拒绝。

paragraphs.en: Another important factor is your environment. Your surroundings can influence your behavior more than you think. If you want to eat healthier, keep fruits visible and hide junk food. If you want to focus on work, put your phone in another room. By designing your environment, you make good habits easier and bad habits harder. This strategy doesn't require willpower—it works because it's convenient.
paragraphs.zh: 另一个重要因素是环境。你的周围环境对你的行为影响比你以为的更大。如果你想吃得健康，把水果放在显眼处，把垃圾食品藏起来。如果你想专注于工作，把手机放在另一个房间。通过设计环境，你可以让好习惯更容易，坏习惯更难。这个策略不需要意志力——它有效是因为方便。

paragraphs.en: Habits also shape our identity. When you practice a habit repeatedly, you start to believe in a new identity. For example, if you run every day, you begin to see yourself as a "runner." This identity then reinforces the habit. To make a habit stick, focus on who you want to become, not just what you want to achieve. Ask yourself: "What would a healthy person do?" and let that mind guide your actions.
paragraphs.zh: 习惯也塑造我们的身份认同。当你反复实践一个习惯时，你开始相信一种新的身份。例如，如果你每天跑步，你开始把自己看作“跑者”。这种身份反过来又强化了习惯。要让习惯持久，关注你想成为什么样的人，而不仅仅是达成什么目标。问问自己：“一个健康的人会怎么做？”让这种心态指导你的行动。

paragraphs.en: Of course, you will face challenges along the way. It's normal to slip up sometimes. The key is not to let one mistake turn into a total collapse. Researchers call this the "don't miss twice" rule: even if you miss a day, make sure you get back on track the next day. Stable progress is more important than perfection. Remember that every small step counts, and consistency is what leads to long-term change.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，在此过程中你会面临挑战。偶尔犯错是正常的。关键是不让一次失误变成全面崩溃。研究人员称之为“不连续错过两次”规则：即使你错过了一天，确保第二天回到正轨。稳定的进步比完美更重要。记住每一步都算数，持续性是带来长期改变的关键。

paragraphs.en: Finally, track your progress. Use a journal or an app to record your habits. Seeing your achievement can boost your motivation. Also, share your goals with friends or join a community of like-minded people. Social support makes habits more enjoyable and sustainable. Remember, the journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step. Start today, and your future self will thank you.
paragraphs.zh: 最后，追踪你的进展。使用日记或应用程序记录你的习惯。看到自己的成就可以提升动力。此外，和朋友分享目标，或加入志同道合的人组成的社区。社会支持让习惯更愉快、更可持续。记住，千里之行始于足下。从今天开始，未来的你会感谢自己。

vocab.def: n. 思想，心态；v. 介意
